root_text.an8.28:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an8.28:1.2: “kati nu kho, sāriputta, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balāni, yehi balehi samannāgato khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an8.28:1.4: “Aṭṭha, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balāni, yehi balehi samannāgato khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an8.28:2.2: Idha, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aniccato sabbe saṅkhārā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti.
root_text.an8.28:2.3: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aniccato sabbe saṅkhārā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an8.28:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aṅgārakāsūpamā kāmā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti.
root_text.an8.28:3.2: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aṅgārakāsūpamā kāmā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an8.28:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno vivekaninnaṁ cittaṁ hoti vivekapoṇaṁ vivekapabbhāraṁ vivekaṭṭhaṁ nekkhammābhirataṁ byantibhūtaṁ sabbaso āsavaṭṭhāniyehi dhammehi.
root_text.an8.28:4.2: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno vivekaninnaṁ cittaṁ hoti vivekapoṇaṁ vivekapabbhāraṁ vivekaṭṭhaṁ nekkhammābhirataṁ byantibhūtaṁ sabbaso āsavaṭṭhāniyehi dhammehi, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an8.28:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvitā honti subhāvitā.
root_text.an8.28:5.2: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvitā honti subhāvitā, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an8.28:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno cattāro iddhipādā bhāvitā honti subhāvitā …pe…
root_text.an8.28:6.4: ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvito hoti subhāvito.
root_text.an8.28:6.5: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvito hoti subhāvito, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an8.28:7.1: Imāni kho, bhante, aṭṭha khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balāni, yehi balehi samannāgato khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:

translation_text.an8.28:1.1: Then Venerable Sāriputta went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an8.28:1.2: “Sāriputta, how many powers does a mendicant who has ended the defilements have that qualify them to claim:
translation_text.an8.28:1.4: “Sir, a mendicant who has ended the defilements has eight powers that qualify them to claim:
translation_text.an8.28:2.2: Firstly, a mendicant with defilements ended has clearly seen with right wisdom all conditions as truly impermanent.
translation_text.an8.28:2.3: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an8.28:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant with defilements ended has clearly seen with right wisdom that sensual pleasures are truly like a pit of glowing coals.
translation_text.an8.28:3.2: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an8.28:4.1: Furthermore, the mind of a mendicant with defilements ended slants, slopes, and inclines to seclusion. They’re withdrawn, loving renunciation, and have totally eliminated defiling influences.
translation_text.an8.28:4.2: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an8.28:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant with defilements ended has well developed the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.an8.28:5.2: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an8.28:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant with defilements ended has well developed the four bases of psychic power …
translation_text.an8.28:6.5: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an8.28:7.1: A mendicant who has ended the defilements possesses these eight powers that qualify them to claim:
